task: Fix OSS hosted-search undated result filtering so Chinese current-day market fallback graph tests accept Tavily/Brave-style transient search results without provider keys or live calls.
securityRequirements: Do not add or expose provider keys, API keys, user credentials, or live provider calls.
Keep the OSS browser/API path keyless.
dependencyRequirements: Do not add packages or lockfile changes.
performanceRequirements: Keep filtering deterministic and local with no extra network calls.
understandingRequirements: Explain why hosted search results can be accepted without explicit published dates.
continuityRequirements: Reuse the existing evidence access hosted-search cache policy and result time filtering patterns.
patternSummary: Reuse evidence_access result time filtering and hosted-search/transient cache policy semantics.
smallestViableChange: Recognize Tavily/Brave-style hosted search metadata as transient hosted search results during relative date filtering, with regression coverage.
assumptions: Hosted search adapters often return fresh ranked results without a published date.
Accepting undated hosted-search results is safer than dropping all evidence for current-day Chinese market questions, as long as no live calls or keys are added.
acceptanceCriteria: The focused Chinese market fallback graph test passes.
Hosted-search result filtering has a direct regression for provider metadata.
OSS remains keyless and no provider execution path or credential field is added.
expectedBehaviorChanges: Relative-date filtering accepts undated hosted-search results when metadata clearly identifies Tavily/Brave-style transient search output.

sources.taskType: 关键词匹配 (65% 置信度)
